title: [Feature]: Add a Grafana Alloy observability connector profile
problem: Grafana Alloy is an OpenTelemetry Collector distribution used to collect and forward telemetry into Grafana and other destinations. nats-sinks currently has Prometheus outputs but no Alloy-specific connector profile or deployment guidance, which can make operators choose between duplicating OTLP work or hand-writing local scrape and forwarding glue.
proposal: Evaluate and implement a Grafana Alloy connector profile that follows the shared observability connector contract. It must be disabled by default, read approved metrics from the local metrics snapshot or shared read-only provider, apply the same observability policy allow and deny lists, use the same stale snapshot, dry-run, timeout, retry, and redaction behavior, and export only policy-approved metric names. The preferred shape should reuse OTLP or Prometheus-compatible handoff and provide Alloy configuration examples rather than inventing a bespoke Grafana API connector.
users: Operators using Grafana Alloy, Grafana Cloud, Mimir, Loki, Tempo, or LGTM-style observability stacks, and maintainers who need a reusable profile model for collector-based deployments.
delivery_semantics: Alloy profile export must remain observational only. Collector restarts, scrape failures, configuration errors, network failures, or rejected exports must not affect ACK, NAK, DLQ, retry, sink writes, or idempotency.
security: The profile must keep sharing disabled until policy review, avoid high-cardinality labels, avoid subjects and mission metadata by default, and document how Alloy service permissions differ from the main nats-sink worker. It must not require access to NATS, Oracle, file-sink output directories, or payload data.
acceptance: Grafana Alloy support is optional and disabled by default.
The profile reuses OTLP or Prometheus-compatible connector behavior where practical.
The profile uses the shared observability policy model and safe metric mapping.
Systemd and Kubernetes guidance keeps Alloy separate from the delivery worker.
Documentation is added as a sub-page under the Observability documentation section.
tests: Add unit tests for generated Alloy profile configuration, disabled policy, allow and deny filtering, stale snapshot handling, dry-run output, safe metric names, and sanitized logging. Add optional integration guidance for a local Alloy process or configuration validation behind markers.
documentation: Add a Grafana Alloy sub-page under the Observability documentation section. The page must include collector architecture, configuration examples, service separation, security defaults, sample output, testing, and limitations.
closeout: Close after the release containing Grafana Alloy profile support is published. Include the release tag, chosen handoff mode, tests run, documentation links, and any follow-up collector deployment issues.
